coredata.dc:title: Experimental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self-ignition of Lithium Ion Battery
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2018 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:issueName: 2017 8th International Conference on Fire Science and Fire Protection Engineering (ICFSFPE 2017)
coredata.dc:creator: Si, Rong-jun
Liu, De-qi
Xue, Shao-qian
coredata.dc:description: This study adopted the external heating method to generate the lithium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched the explosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly inhibit the explosion of the lithium ion battery through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature and weaken the explosive strength. The combustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage.
coredata.dcterms:subject: lithium ion batteries
fire suppression
explosion suppression
gas extinguishing agent
combustion temperature

xocs:title-editors-group.ce:title: 2017 8th International Conference on Fire Science and Fire Protection Engineering (ICFSFPE 2017)

xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: © 2018 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.

xocs:cp-license-lines.xocs:cp-license-line: This is an open access article under the CC BY-NC-ND license.

xocs:cp-notices.xocs:cp-notice: © 2018 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: ScienceDirect Available online at www.sciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 1877-7058 Â© 2018 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Peer-review under responsibility of the organizing committee of ICFSFPE 2017 10.1016/j.proeng.2017.12.057 2017 8th International Conference on Fire Science and Fire Protection Engineering (on the Development of Performance-based Fire Code) 10.1016/j.proeng.2017.12.057 Â© 2018 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Peer-review under responsibility of the organizing committee of ICFSFPE 2017. 1877-7058 Available online at www.sciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Experimental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self- ignition of Lithium Ion Battery Rong-jun SI*, De-qi LIU, Shao-qian XUE China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China Abstract This study adopted the external heating method to generate the lithium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched the explosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly inhibit the explosion of the lithium ion battery through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature and weaken the explosive strength. The combustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage. Â© 2017 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Selection and peer-review under responsibility of the Academic Committee of ICFSFPE 2017. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosion suppression, gas extinguishing agent, combustion temperature Lithium ion battery have been widely used in new energy vehicles. In recent years, the occurrence of new energy vehicles explosion accident makes its security issues have been widespread concern and attention. Lithium ion battery is at risk of burning and even exploding in extreme cases such as excess charging, collisions and high temperatures, and they are difficult to put out. Both HFC-227ea and CO2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, which is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, HFC-227ea and CO2 are used as the explosion suppression media to study the effect of the explosion suppression of lithium ion batteries. 1. Analysis of lithium ion battery combustion mechanism and explosion suppression mechanism 1.1. Analysis of combustion process and characteristics of lithium ion battery The lithium ion battery mainly consists of positive pole, negative pole, diaphragm, electrolyte, shell and other components. The positive material is always intercalated transition metal oxides or polyanionic compounds, the negative material is mostly made of graphite, and the electrolyte is mainly composed of organic mixed solution and lithium salt. The diaphragm acts as a barrier between the positive and the negative, preventing electrons from passing through which cause short circuit and allowing ions to pass through the electrolyte. When the battery is used, Carbon negative electrode, positive electrode active material and electrolyte will produce electrochemical or chemical reaction and give off heat. The temperature of the battery will be raised, which will further promote the reaction. However, in the case of short circuit, high temperature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel.: +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73@163.com il l li t . i ir t. r i i ri ( ) i l i i ri r i s r I stit t t , i , i t t i t t t t r l ti t t r t t lit i i tt r t ti , r i - 2 t t fir r i l i t t, r r t l i r i ff t f t r i lit i i tt r . r lt t t - 2 i l i i it t l i f t lit i i tt r t r t t f li . - 2 ti i t fl , r t ti t r t r t l i tr t . ti t r t r f lit i i tt r r t r t : i iti l r t , r ri . It i r t t r t ti i fir l i i t i iti l r t t . t . li l i t . l ti i i ilit t i itt . r s: lit i i tt ri s, fir s r ssi , l si s r ssi , s ti is i t, sti t r t r it i i tt i l i i l . t , t i l l i i t it it i i tt ti . it i i tt i t i i l i i t i , lli i i t t , t i i lt t t t. t l t t ti i i t, i i t i i ti . , t l i i i t t t t t l i i lit i i tt i . . . . l i f ti t i ti f lit i i tt lit i i tt i l i t iti l , ti l , i , l t l t , ll t t . iti t i l i l i t l t t iti t l i l i i , t ti t i l i tl it , t l t l t i i l i i l ti lit i lt. i t i t t iti t ti , ti l t i t i t i it ll i i t t t l t l t . t tt i , ti l t , iti l t ti t i l l t l t ill l t i l i l ti i t. t t t tt ill i , i ill t t t ti . , i t t i it, i t t , lli i t , t tt i i rr s i t r. l.: - ; f : - - . - il r ss: sirj . Available online at .sciencedirect.co Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 i t l t i l i i f lf- i iti f it i tt -j I , e- i I , a - ia China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China bstract This study adopted the external heating ethod to generate the lithiu ion battery spontaneous co bustion, spraying FC-227ea and C 2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched the explosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithiu ion battery. The results sho that FC-227ea and C 2 ainly inhibit the explosion of the lithiu ion battery through the ethod of cooling. The FC-227ea and C 2 can extinguish the open fla e, reduce the co bustion te perature and eaken the explosive strength. The co bustion te perature of lithiu ion battery undergoes three stages: initial gro th, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take easures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial gro th stage. 2017 The uthors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Selection and peer-revie under responsibility of the cade ic o ittee of I FSFPE 2017. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosion suppression, gas extinguishing agent, combustion temperature Lithiu ion battery have been idely used in ne energy vehicles. In recent years, the occurrence of ne energy vehicles explosion accident akes its security issues have been idespread concern and attention. Lithiu ion battery is at risk of burning and even exploding in extre e cases such as excess charging, collisions and high te peratures, and they are difficult to put out. oth F -227ea and 2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, hich is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, F -227ea and 2 are used as the explosion suppression edia to study the effect of the explosion suppression of lithiu ion batteries. 1. alysis of lit i io attery co stio ec a is a ex losio s ressio ec a is 1.1. Analysis of co bustion process and characteristics of lithiu ion battery The lithiu ion battery ainly consists of positive pole, negative pole, diaphrag , electrolyte, shell and other co ponents. The positive aterial is al ays intercalated transition etal oxides or polyanionic co pounds, the negative aterial is ostly ade of graphite, and the electrolyte is ainly co posed of organic ixed solution and lithiu salt. The diaphrag acts as a barrier bet een the positive and the negative, preventing electrons fro passing through hich cause short circuit and allo ing ions to pass through the electrolyte. hen the battery is used, arbon negative electrode, positive electrode active aterial and electrolyte ill produce electroche ical or che ical reaction and give off heat. The te perature of the battery ill be raised, hich ill further pro ote the reaction. o ever, in the case of short circuit, high te perature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel.: +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73 163.com Available online at www.sciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Experimental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self- ignition of Lithium Ion Battery Rong-jun SI*, De-qi LIU, Shao-qian XUE China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China Abstract This study adopted the external heating me hod to gener te the lithium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched he explosion suppr ssion effect of he gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 m inly inhibit the explosion f the lithium ion batt ry through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature an weaken th explosive str ngth. The c mbustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage. Â© 2017 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Selection and peer-review under responsibility of the Academic Committee of ICFSFPE 2017. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosion suppression, gas extinguishing agent, combustion temperature Lith um ion bat ery have been widely used in new energy vehicles. In recent years, the occurrence of new energy vehicles explosion accident makes its securi y issues have been widespread concern and attention. Lithiu ion battery is at risk of burning and even exploding in extreme cases such as excess charging, collisions and high temperatures, and they are difficult to put out. Both HFC-227ea and CO2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, which is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, HFC-227ea and CO2 are used as the explosion suppression media to study the effect of the explosion suppression of lithium ion batteries. 1. Analysis of lithium ion battery combustion mechanism and explosion suppression mechanism 1.1. Analysis of combustion process and characteristics of lithium ion battery The lithium ion battery main y consists of positive pole, negative pole, diaphragm, electrolyte, shell and o her components. The positive material is always intercalated transition metal oxides or polyanionic compounds, the negative material is mostly made of graphite, and the electrolyte is mainly composed of organic mixed solution and lithium salt. The diaphragm acts as a barrier be ween the positive and he negative, preventing electrons from passing through which cause short circuit and allowing ions to pass through the electrolyte. When the battery is used, Carbon negat ve electrode, positive electrode active materia and electrolyte will produce electrochemical or chemical reaction and give off heat. The temperature of the battery will be raised, which will further promote the reaction. However, in the case of short circuit, high temperature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel : +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73@163.com Available online at www.sciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Experi ental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self- ignition of Lithiu Ion Battery Rong-jun SI*, De-qi LIU, Shao-qian XUE China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China Abstract This study adopted the external heating method to generate the lithium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched the explosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly inhibit the explosion f the lithium ion battery through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature and weaken the explosive strength. The c mbustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage. Â© 2017 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Selection and peer-review under responsibility of the Academic Committee of ICFSFPE 2017. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosion suppression, gas extinguishing agent, combustion temperature Lith um ion bat ery have been widely us d in new ergy vehicles. I r cent ye rs, the occurrence f new nergy vehicles explosion accid t makes its securi y issues have been widespread concern and attention. Lithium ion battery is at risk of burning and even exploding in extreme cases such as excess charging, collisio s and high temperatures, and they are difficult to put out. Both HFC-227 a and CO2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, w ich is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, HFC-227ea and CO2 are used as the explosion suppression media to study the effect of the explosion suppression of lithium ion batteries. 1. Analysis of lithium ion battery combustion mechanism and explosion suppression mechanism 1.1. Analysis of combustion process and characteristics of lithium ion battery The lithium ion batt ry main y consist of positive pole, negative pole, diaphragm, electrolyte, shell and o her components. The positive material is always intercalated tr nsition metal oxides or polyanionic c mpounds, the negativ m teri l is mo tly made of graphit , and the electrolyte is mainly composed of organic mixed solution and lit ium salt. Th diaphragm acts as a barrier be ween the positive and he n gative, preventing electrons from passing through which cause short circuit and allowing ions to pass through th lectrolyte. When the battery is used, Carbon negat electrode, positive electrode active materia and electrolyte will produce electr chemical or chemical reaction and give off heat. The temp ratu e of the battery will be aised, w ich will further promote the reaction. However, in the case of short circuit, high temperature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel.: +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73@163.com Available online at www.sciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Experimental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self- ignition of Lithium Ion Battery Ron -jun SI*, De-qi LIU, Shao-qian XUE China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China Ab tract This study adopted t e external heating method to generate the lithium i n battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion t st, a d res arched the xplosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly inhibit the explosion of the lithium ion battery through the method of cooling. Th HFC-227ea CO2 can extinguish the pen fl me, reduce the combustion temperature and weaken the explosive strength. The combustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage. Â© 2017 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosion suppression, gas extinguishing agent, combustion temperature Lith um ion bat ery have bee widely us d in new ergy vehicles. I r cent ye rs, the occurrence f new nergy vehicles explosion accid makes its securi y issues hav been widespread concern and attention. L thiu ion battery is at isk of burning and even exploding in extreme cases such as excess charging, collisio s and high temperatu , a d they are difficult to put out. Both HFC-227 a and CO2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, w ich is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, HFC-227ea and CO2 are used as the explosion suppression media to study the effect of the explosion suppression of lithium ion batteries. Anal of lithium ion battery combu on mechanism and explosion suppression mechanism 1.1. Analys s of c mbustion process and characteristics of lithium ion battery The lithium ion batt ry main y consist of positive pole, negativ pole, diaphragm, electrolyte, shell and o her components. The positive material is always intercalated tr nsition metal oxides o polyanionic c mpounds, the negativ m teri l is mo tly made of graphit , and the electrolyte is mainly composed of organic mixed solution and lit ium salt. Th diaphragm acts as a barrier be ween the positive and h n gative, pr venting electrons from passing through hich ca s short circuit and allowing ions to pass through th lectrolyte. When t e b ttery is used, Carbon negat electrode, positive electrode a tive materia and electrolyt will produc electr chemical or chemical reaction and give off heat. The temp ratu e of the battery will be aised, w ich will further promote the reaction. However, in the case of short circuit, high temperature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel.: +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73@163.com Available online at www.sciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Experi ental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self- ignitio of Lithiu Ion B ttery Ron -jun SI*, De-qi LIU, Shao-qian XUE China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China Abstract This study adopted the external heating method to generate the lithium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched the explosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly inhibit the explosion of the lithium ion battery through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature and weaken the explosive strength. The combustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage. Â© 2017 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Selection and peer-review under responsibility of the Academic Committee of ICFSFPE 2017. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosion suppression, gas extinguishing agent, combustion temperature Lith um ion bat ery have bee widely us d in new ergy vehicles. I r cent ye rs, the occurrence f new nergy vehicles explosion accid t makes its securi y issues hav been widespread concern and attention. Lithiu ion battery is at risk of burning and even exploding in extreme cases such as excess charging, collisio s and high temperatur s, a d they are difficult to put out. Both HFC-227 a and CO2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, w ich is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, HFC-227ea and CO2 are used as the explosion suppression media to study the effect of the explosion suppression of lithium ion batteries. . Anal i of lithium ion battery combu tion mechanism and explosion suppression mechanism 1.1. Analysis of c mbustion process and characteristics of lithium ion battery The lithium ion batt ry main y consist of positive pole, negativ pole, diaphragm, electrolyte, shell and o her components. The positive material is always intercalated tr nsition metal oxides or polyanionic c mpounds, the negativ m teri l is mo tly made of graphit , and the electrolyte is mainly composed of organic mixed solution and lit ium salt. Th diaphragm acts as a barrier be ween the positive and h n gative, pr venting electrons from passing through which ca s short circuit and allowing ions to pass through th lectrolyte. When t e b ttery is used, Carbon negat electrode, positive electrode a tive materia and electrolyt will produc electr chemical or chemical reaction and give off heat. The temp ratu e of the battery will be aised, w ich will further promote the reaction. However, in the case of short circuit, high temperature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel.: +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73@163.com Available online . ciencedirect.com Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Experimental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self- ignitio of Lithium Ion Battery Ron -jun SI*, De-qi LIU, Shao-qian XUE China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China Abstract is study adopted the exter al heating method to gener te the li ium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to c duct fi e suppression explosion test, and r ea ched he explosion suppr ssion effect of he gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea nd CO2 m inly inhibit the explosion f the lithium ion batt ry through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature an weaken th explosive str ngth. The c mbustion temperature of lithium ion batt ry undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp ri e a d deca . It is necessary to take easures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage. Â© 2017 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd. Sel ct n and peer-review under re ponsibility of the Academic Committee of ICFSFPE 2017. Keywords: lithium ion batteries, fire suppression, explosio suppression, gas xtinguishing agent, combu tion temperature Lith um ion bat ery have bee widely us d in new ergy vehicles. I r ce t ye rs, the o currence f new ergy vehicles explosion cci m kes its securi y issues hav been widespread concern and at ention. L thiu ion battery is at isk of burning and even exploding in extreme cases such as excess charging, collisio s and high temperatu , a d they are difficult to put out. Both HFC-227 a and CO2 belong to the gas extinguishing agent, w ich is fast fire-fighting. Therefore, HFC-227ea and CO2 are used as the explosion suppression medi to study the ffect of the ex losion suppression of lithium ion batteries. Anal of lithium ion battery combu on mechanism and explosion suppression mechanism 1.1. Analys s of c mbustion process and characteristics of lithium ion battery T e lithium ion tt ry main y consist of positive pole, negativ pol , diaphragm, electrolyte, shell and o her components. The p sitive material i always intercalated r nsition metal oxides o polyanionic c mpounds, the negativ m teri l is mo tly made of graphit , and the el ctrolyte is mainly compose of organic mixed solution and lit ium salt. Th diaphragm a ts as a barr er be ween the positive and h n gative, pr venting electrons from passing through hich ca s sh rt circuit and allowing ions to pass through th le t oly e. When t e b ttery is used, Carbon negat electrode, positive electrode a tive materia and electrolyt will produc electr chemical or chemical reaction and give off heat. The temp ratu e of the battery will be aised, w ich will further promote the reaction. However, in the case of short circuit, high temperature, collision and other abuse, the battery inside * Corresponding author. Tel.: +86-13709425791; fax: +86-23-65239246. E-mail address: sirj73@163.com 630 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 easy to cause thermal runaway, the rapid release of energy leads to electrolyte combustion, and the temperature of the battery rises rapidly Because other components of the lithium ion battery are all burning substances [1], such as graphite anode, diaphragm, cathode, which causes the battery materials to burn and even explode. After the battery shell is broken, the intense oxidation of air with lithium also causes the battery to burn and even explode [2]. When lithium ion batteries burn, the cathode material breaks down and releases O2, and the battery combustion will also release CO and other combustible gases [3]. The large amount of heat released by internal reaction can also provide energy for lithium ion battery combustion. These factors also make lithium ion battery burn even in confined environments. Once burned, it is extremely difficult to extinguish. 1.2. Analysis on attenuation mechanism of gas suppressor HFC-227ea is colorless, almost tasteless and low toxic gas at normal temperature and atmospheric pressure, with good thermal stability and chemical stability. At 20â„ƒ, the saturated vapor pressure of HFC-227ea is 0.391MPa. HFC-227ea realizes the fire suppression and explosion suppression through chemical inhibition and physical method, such as reducing the oxygen concentration, absorbing heat. Liquid HFC-227ea will rapidly vaporized and expanded volume after being ejected, which reduces the oxygen concentration. The vaporization will absorb large amounts of heat that sharply reduce the reaction temperature of combustion and combustion reaction activity, Thus the liquid HFC-227ea can play a role of fire suppression and explosion suppression. At the same time, HFC-227ea pyrolysis will produce free radicals and react with the active radicals such as O, H, OH and other reactive radicals during the combustion reaction, thereby, the chemical chain reaction in the combustion process is interrupted and the combustion reaction is suppressed [4]. CO2 is a colorless and odorless gas at normal temperature, with low toxic, it will not support combustion. At 20â„ƒ, the saturated vapor pressure of CO2 is 5.72MPa, which belongs to high pressure liquefied gas. The mechanism of fire and explosion suppression of CO2 mainly has the effect of suffocation and cooling: The CO2 is stored in a liquid form in a cylinder and rapidly vaporized after being ejected, which produces a large amount of CO2 in the process of vaporization to reduce the concentration of O2; the process of vaporization needs to absorb a lot of heat. At the same time, because of the enthalpy drop, the temperature of CO2 decreases sharply to form fine solid dry ice particles, and the dry ice absorbs its surrounding heat and sublimation, so the CO2 has a cooling effect. 2. Test method and apparatus for fire suppression and explosion suppression 2.1. Test arrangement The box body of the Self-made test cabinet is 380mmÃ—280 mmÃ—375 mm, the front of box is provided with an observation window, the window size is 150mmÃ—150mm. The rear side of the box is provided with a thermocouple mounting interface, and the pressure sensor installation interface is arranged at the top and the two sides. In the test, 3 thermocouples are installed on the back of the box, the installation position is 10cm above the battery, and the thermocouple is connected with a paperless recorder. A pressure sensor is installed on the left and right sides of the upper part of the test case, which is connected with the data acquisition system to monitor the explosion pressure in the test chamber. During the test, the infrared thermometer is used to monitor the surface temperature of the battery. The test mainly monitors the burning temperature of the lithium ion battery and the explosion pressure in the box. The combustion temperature is monitored by armored K-type thermocouples, the monitoring temperature range is 0â„ƒ to 1000â„ƒ. The thermocouple monitoring value was recorded using a paperless recorder, and the recording interval was 2s. Through the pressure sensor and data acquisition system to collect the value of the explosion chamber pressure, the pressure sensor measuring range 0~30KPa; the infrared thermometer can monitor the range -20â„ƒ~550â„ƒto monitor the surface temperature of lithium ion batteries. 2.2. Experiment method The test battery is ternary polymer electric vehicle lithium ion battery which is removed shell, the ternary polymer material is the lithium nickel cobalt manganese, which use nickel salt, cobalt salt, manganese salt as raw material, the ratio of nickel salt, cobalt salt, manganese salt can be adjusted, and the negative material is graphite. In the Test, the rated voltage of the battery is 48V, and the rated current of the battery is 10A, the state of charge for the factory power, the size of battery is 285Ã—92Ã—72mm. The external heating method is used to make the lithium ion battery spontaneously. By heating the bottom of the test box, the battery is heated continuously. When the battery is on fire, the fuel under the tank is removed, and the bottom plate of the box body is stopped. Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 631 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 easy to cause thermal runaway, the rapid release of energy leads to electrolyte combustion, and the temperature of the battery rises rapidly Because other components of the lithium ion battery are all burning substances [1], such as graphite anode, diaphragm, cathode, which causes the battery materials to burn and even explode. After the battery shell is broken, the intense oxidation of air with lithium also causes the battery to burn and even explode [2]. When lithium ion batteries burn, the cathode material breaks down and releases O2, and the battery combustion will also release CO and other combustible gases [3]. The large amount of heat released by internal reaction can also provide energy for lithium ion battery combustion. These factors also make lithium ion battery burn even in confined environments. Once burned, it is extremely difficult to extinguish. 1.2. Analysis on attenuation mechanism of gas suppressor HFC-227ea is colorless, almost tasteless and low toxic gas at normal temperature and atmospheric pressure, with good thermal stability and chemical stability. At 20â„ƒ, the saturated vapor pressure of HFC-227ea is 0.391MPa. HFC-227ea realizes the fire suppression and explosion suppression through chemical inhibition and physical method, such as reducing the oxygen concentration, absorbing heat. Liquid HFC-227ea will rapidly vaporized and expanded volume after being ejected, which reduces the oxygen concentration. The vaporization will absorb large amounts of heat that sharply reduce the reaction temperature of combustion and combustion reaction activity, Thus the liquid HFC-227ea can play a role of fire suppression and explosion suppression. At the same time, HFC-227ea pyrolysis will produce free radicals and react with the active radicals such as O, H, OH and other reactive radicals during the combustion reaction, thereby, the chemical chain reaction in the combustion process is interrupted and the combustion reaction is suppressed [4]. CO2 is a colorless and odorless gas at normal temperature, with low toxic, it will not support combustion. At 20â„ƒ, the saturated vapor pressure of CO2 is 5.72MPa, which belongs to high pressure liquefied gas. The mechanism of fire and explosion suppression of CO2 mainly has the effect of suffocation and cooling: The CO2 is stored in a liquid form in a cylinder and rapidly vaporized after being ejected, which produces a large amount of CO2 in the process of vaporization to reduce the concentration of O2; the process of vaporization needs to absorb a lot of heat. At the same time, because of the enthalpy drop, the temperature of CO2 decreases sharply to form fine solid dry ice particles, and the dry ice absorbs its surrounding heat and sublimation, so the CO2 has a cooling effect. 2. Test method and apparatus for fire suppression and explosion suppression 2.1. Test arrangement The box body of the Self-made test cabinet is 380mmÃ—280 mmÃ—375 mm, the front of box is provided with an observation window, the window size is 150mmÃ—150mm. The rear side of the box is provided with a thermocouple mounting interface, and the pressure sensor installation interface is arranged at the top and the two sides. In the test, 3 thermocouples are installed on the back of the box, the installation position is 10cm above the battery, and the thermocouple is connected with a paperless recorder. A pressure sensor is installed on the left and right sides of the upper part of the test case, which is connected with the data acquisition system to monitor the explosion pressure in the test chamber. During the test, the infrared thermometer is used to monitor the surface temperature of the battery. The test mainly monitors the burning temperature of the lithium ion battery and the explosion pressure in the box. The combustion temperature is monitored by armored K-type thermocouples, the monitoring temperature range is 0â„ƒ to 1000â„ƒ. The thermocouple monitoring value was recorded using a paperless recorder, and the recording interval was 2s. Through the pressure sensor and data acquisition system to collect the value of the explosion chamber pressure, the pressure sensor measuring range 0~30KPa; the infrared thermometer can monitor the range -20â„ƒ~550â„ƒto monitor the surface temperature of lithium ion batteries. 2.2. Experiment method The test battery is ternary polymer electric vehicle lithium ion battery which is removed shell, the ternary polymer material is the lithium nickel cobalt manganese, which use nickel salt, cobalt salt, manganese salt as raw material, the ratio of nickel salt, cobalt salt, manganese salt can be adjusted, and the negative material is graphite. In the Test, the rated voltage of the battery is 48V, and the rated current of the battery is 10A, the state of charge for the factory power, the size of battery is 285Ã—92Ã—72mm. The external heating method is used to make the lithium ion battery spontaneously. By heating the bottom of the test box, the battery is heated continuously. When the battery is on fire, the fuel under the tank is removed, and the bottom plate of the box body is stopped. Rong-jun SI et al / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 The self-made explosion-proof device is used as the HFC-227ea test jet distributor. The size of the explosion-proof device is 300Ã—310mm, the starting voltage of the explosion-proof device is 12V, and the response time of the explosion- proof device is less than 1s. HFC-227ea has lower saturated vapor pressure at ambient temperature and filled with N2 to improve storage pressure. The CO2 spray test device consists of CO2 cylinders, pressure reducing valves, nozzles and shower nozzles. The test nozzle is fixed on the top of the test case and faces the upper surface of the battery. It is about 20cm of the distance from the battery. Fig. 1.Test schematic arrangement 3. Test process and analysis of fire suppression and explosion suppression 3.1. Combustion test and analysis of lithium ion battery When the lithium ion battery test first appears naked flame, the surface temperature of the battery is about 205â„ƒ that is measured by infrared thermometer; after the fire, the burning of the battery is very violent. The burning flame is filled with the whole test chamber and is ejected from the observation window. Thermocouples measure the maximum temperature in the test chamber at 705â„ƒ, as shown in Table 1. Table 1. Test results of lithium ion battery combustion Test serial number The temperature of the battery when the flame appears (â„ƒ) Maximum temperature (â„ƒ) Maximum pressure (KPa) Remarks 1 205 705 - Fig. 2. Combustion temperature curve of lithium ion battery The temperature curve of the combustion temperature with the time monitored in the test chamber is shown in Fig. 2. It can be divided into the initial growth stage, the rapid rising stage and the decay stage. In the initial growth stage, the temperature of the test chamber increases slowly, which is affected by the external heating and the lithium ion battery heat release. During this stage, the battery casing is damaged, and smoke is generated inside the battery. When the lithium ion 632 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 battery produces an open flame, the combustion temperature goes up sharply, the lithium ion battery burns, and the explosion occurs from time to time during the combustion, with a loud sound that can be generated by a burst, reaching as far as 4m. Thereafter, the temperature begins to decrease and goes into the decay stage. A large amount of heat generated by the reaction inside the battery is released instantaneously, resulting in an explosion in the combustion process, as a result of the open fire, the test box window is removed from the observation window, and the observation window provides a pressure relief outlet for the explosion pressure, so the pressure collection system fails to detect the explosion pressure signal. 3.2. Test and analysis of fire suppression and explosion suppression of HFC-227ea The results of the explosion suppression test of HFC-227ea are shown in Table 2. Table 2. the results of the explosion suppression test of HFC-227ea Test serial number Quality of suppressant (kg) Storage pressure (MPa) The temperature of the battery when the fire is present (â„ƒ) Spraying time (s) Maximum temperature (â„ƒ) Phenomenon 1 2.6 2.4 210 80 604 The fire rekindled after the spraying about 3s 2 2.4 2.5 220 500 427 There is no constant fire 3 2.6 2.5 210 470 396 There is no constant fire After the fire, the lithium ion battery is sprayed HFC-227ea immediately in test 1, the fire is soon extinguished, but the fire rekindles after the spraying about 3s, the lithium ion battery burns violently. In order to investigate the phenomenon of flaring in the test 1, test 2 and 3 reduce the flow rate and prolong the spraying time. When the fire in the test box is present, the HFC-227ea is continuously sprayed. During the spraying process, the lithium ion battery continues to react until the lithium ion battery is burned and no sustained flame is produced. In test 1, after being sprayed with HFC-227ea, the lithium ion battery explodes with a loud sound, and the maximum distance of the projectile is about 4m. In the test 2 and 3, the explosive strength of the lithium ion battery is significantly reduced, we can still hear the sound of lithium ion battery explosion, but no long-range projectiles. The liquid HFC-227ea absorbs heat and cools the combustion reaction temperature to extinguish the burning flame of the lithium ion battery. In test 1, the HFC-227ea spray time is short and the flow can be spread from the observation window to the outside, although the flame is quickly extinguished, the chemical reaction inside the lithium ion battery continues, so the flame will soon develop again when the spray is stopped. In the test 2 and 3, the HFC-227ea continuously absorbs the heat during the entire battery combustion because of the continuous spraying of the antiknock agent. Therefore, after the fire is extinguished, no continuous fire is observed in the entire combustion stage of the battery. The fire and explosion suppression mechanism of HFC-227ea also includes reducing oxygen concentration and chemical inhibition methods. Because the lithium ion battery releases oxygen when burning, it cannot effectively reduce the oxygen concentration by spraying HFC-227ea. The internal heat radiation of the lithium ion battery results in a chain heat reaction and generates OH radicals [2], because the lithium ion battery is a complex structure, and the battery surface with a packaging, making the HFC-227ea difficult to affect the internal chemical reaction of the battery, the reaction inside the battery does not stop until the fuel is burned in the lithium ion battery in the test. The temperature variation curves of thermocouple monitoring are shown in Fig. 3, Fig. 4 and Fig. 5. Fig. 3. The temperature curve of HFC-227ea in test 1 Fig. 4. The temperature curve of HFC-227ea in test 2 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 633 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 battery produces an open flame, the combustion temperature goes up sharply, the lithium ion battery burns, and the explosion occurs from time to time during the combustion, with a loud sound that can be generated by a burst, reaching as far as 4m. Thereafter, the temperature begins to decrease and goes into the decay stage. A large amount of heat generated by the reaction inside the battery is released instantaneously, resulting in an explosion in the combustion process, as a result of the open fire, the test box window is removed from the observation window, and the observation window provides a pressure relief outlet for the explosion pressure, so the pressure collection system fails to detect the explosion pressure signal. 3.2. Test and analysis of fire suppression and explosion suppression of HFC-227ea The results of the explosion suppression test of HFC-227ea are shown in Table 2. Table 2. the results of the explosion suppression test of HFC-227ea Test serial number Quality of suppressant (kg) Storage pressure (MPa) The temperature of the battery when the fire is present (â„ƒ) Spraying time (s) Maximum temperature (â„ƒ) Phenomenon 1 2.6 2.4 210 80 604 The fire rekindled after the spraying about 3s 2 2.4 2.5 220 500 427 There is no constant fire 3 2.6 2.5 210 470 396 There is no constant fire After the fire, the lithium ion battery is sprayed HFC-227ea immediately in test 1, the fire is soon extinguished, but the fire rekindles after the spraying about 3s, the lithium ion battery burns violently. In order to investigate the phenomenon of flaring in the test 1, test 2 and 3 reduce the flow rate and prolong the spraying time. When the fire in the test box is present, the HFC-227ea is continuously sprayed. During the spraying process, the lithium ion battery continues to react until the lithium ion battery is burned and no sustained flame is produced. In test 1, after being sprayed with HFC-227ea, the lithium ion battery explodes with a loud sound, and the maximum distance of the projectile is about 4m. In the test 2 and 3, the explosive strength of the lithium ion battery is significantly reduced, we can still hear the sound of lithium ion battery explosion, but no long-range projectiles. The liquid HFC-227ea absorbs heat and cools the combustion reaction temperature to extinguish the burning flame of the lithium ion battery. In test 1, the HFC-227ea spray time is short and the flow can be spread from the observation window to the outside, although the flame is quickly extinguished, the chemical reaction inside the lithium ion battery continues, so the flame will soon develop again when the spray is stopped. In the test 2 and 3, the HFC-227ea continuously absorbs the heat during the entire battery combustion because of the continuous spraying of the antiknock agent. Therefore, after the fire is extinguished, no continuous fire is observed in the entire combustion stage of the battery. The fire and explosion suppression mechanism of HFC-227ea also includes reducing oxygen concentration and chemical inhibition methods. Because the lithium ion battery releases oxygen when burning, it cannot effectively reduce the oxygen concentration by spraying HFC-227ea. The internal heat radiation of the lithium ion battery results in a chain heat reaction and generates OH radicals [2], because the lithium ion battery is a complex structure, and the battery surface with a packaging, making the HFC-227ea difficult to affect the internal chemical reaction of the battery, the reaction inside the battery does not stop until the fuel is burned in the lithium ion battery in the test. The temperature variation curves of thermocouple monitoring are shown in Fig. 3, Fig. 4 and Fig. 5. Fig. 3. The temperature curve of HFC-227ea in test 1 Fig. 4. The temperature curve of HFC-227ea in test 2 Rong-jun SI et al / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Fig. 5. The temperature curve of HFC-227ea in test 3 The combustion temperature changes in the three tests are recorded in Fig. 3, Fig. 4 and Fig. 5 respectively. In test 1, because of the short time of HFC-227ea spraying, the burning temperature of lithium ion battery still experiences a sharp increase stage after the spraying, and the temperature peak is 605â„ƒ. In the test 2 and 3, the temperature in the test chamber can be divided into three stages, the initial growth stage, the temperature rising stage and the attenuation stage. As the flame appears, the explosive is continuously sprayed, so the temperature rising rate decreases during the temperature rising stage, in the twice test, the temperature monitored by thermocouple is rising in fluctuation, and the monitored temperature peaks are 427â„ƒ and 396â„ƒ, respectively. Compared with test 1 and lithium ion battery combustion test, the temperature peak decreases obviously. In test 2 and 3, the batterries have entered the stage of rapid temperature rise before and after the fire is generated. At this time, the thermal runaway inside the battery have been exacerbated, and the heat release rate of the battery is very fast, so that the peak temperature in the test is still high. However, compared with the test 1, the absorption and cooling effect of HFC-227ea make the cells in the test 2 and 3 not appear violent combustion, and the explosion intensity is also weakened. In the initial stage of temperature increase, the temperature and temperature rising rate in the test chamber are mainly influenced by the external heating state, so in the initial growth stage, the temperature rise rate rapidly fast in test 2 and 3, and the observation window still hasn't been screened, heat can pass test box window in and out of the box body, therefore temperature fluctuate in the initial growth phase. 3.3. Test and analysis of fire suppression and explosion suppression of CO2 In the test, the outlet pressure of the CO2 sprinkler valve is adjusted to 0.5MPa. When the battery burns open fire, the valve is opened manually to start spraying CO2. In the process of spraying CO2, there is no continuous flame, but the lithium ion battery continues to explode from time to time, but the intensity is weakened and only the sound is heard, no long-range projectiles. The results of the CO2 suppression test are shown in Table 3. Table 3. The results of the explosion suppression test of CO2 Test serial number The temperature of the battery when the fire is present (â„ƒ) Spraying time (s) Maximum temperature (â„ƒ) 1 220 450 323 2 205 462 345 Because the battery releases oxygen when it is burning, the suffocating effect of CO2 is not enough to suppress a lithium ion battery. Therefore, CO2 extinguish the fire of lithium ion battery mainly rely on heat absorption cooling effect, by absorbing the heat released by the reaction inside the battery, put out the flame, and reduce the temperature inside the test case. In the test, the peak temperature in the box is reduced, and the peak temperature are 323â„ƒ and 345â„ƒ, however, there is still a temperature rise during the spraying process. In the test, the heat generation rate of the lithium ion battery combustion reaction is still greater than the heat loss rate, which keeps the temperature rising. The combustion temperature changes in the chamber as shown in Fig. 6 and Fig. 7. 634 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Fig. 6. The temperature curve of CO2 in test 1 Fig. 7. The temperature curve of CO2 in test 2 3.4. Analysis on fire suppression and explosion suppression of self-ignition of lithium ion battery Because the battery burns out the oxygen, the suffocation method does not prevent the lithium ion batteries from burning, and the internal chemical reaction of the battery is less affected by the fire extinguishing agent, the HFC-227ea chemical reaction based on the chain reaction fails to prevent the battery combustion reaction. However, the cooling mechanism of the two kinds of inhibitors play a certain role in suppressing the explosion of lithium ion batteries, When the lithium ion battery is sprayed with a suppressant, the flame is extinguished, the peak of the temperature and the rising speed of the temperature in the test chamber decreased in different degrees. At the same time, the cooling effect of HFC-227ea and CO2 weaken the combustion and explosion strength of the battery. The latent heat of vaporization of HFC-227ea is 132.5kJ/kg, and the latent heat of vaporization of CO2 is 347kJ/kg. It is found that the heat absorbed by CO2 vaporization is much larger than that of HFC-227ea vaporization, and the dry ice sublimation also needs to absorb a lot of heat, and the heat absorption capacity of CO2 is stronger, which makes the cooling effect of CO2 significantly. Inhibition of lithium ion battery explosion should be used to absorb heat-resistant extinguishing agent, such as CO2 fire extinguishing agent, and the temperature of the test chamber begins to enter a sharp rising stage before and after the combustion of the battery occurs, the rate of chemical reaction in the battery is very fast, and the heat is released rapidly. It is difficult to take a good cooling effect when the fire extinguishing measures are taken. Therefore, it is advisable to take measures to extinguish the fire at the initial stage of temperature increase. 4. Conclusion HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly rely on cooling effect to restrain the explosion of lithium ion battery. HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the flame of lithium ion battery, reduce the burning temperature and decrease the explosion strength. Moreover, the cooling effect of CO2 is better than that of HFC-227ea, so the CO2 extinguishing agent with high heat absorption ability should be selected to restrain the spontaneous combustion of lithium ion battery. After the lithium ion battery is heated, the temperature will experience the initial growth stage, the sharp rising stage and the attenuation stage, so it is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion at the initial stage of the initial temperature increase. Acknowledgement This work was financially supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (51504285) References [1] Gao F., Yang K., Li DH., 2015. Evaluation of Combustibility of Lithium Ion Battery Components and Dangers They Involve, China Safety Science Journay 25, pp. 62-66. [2] Chen YH., Tang ZY., Li DH., 2006. Research of Explosion Mechanism of Lithium-Ion Battery, Progress in Chemistry 18, pp. 823-831. [3] Ping P., 2014. Lithium Ion Battery Thermal Runaway and Fire Risk Analysis and The Development on The Safer Battery System, University of Science and Technology of China. [4] Wei SW., Jiang XS., He B., 2016. Experimental Study on Suppression of Gasoline-air Mixture Explosion in Narrow and Long Confined Space by Heptafluoropropane 12, pp. 128-133. [5] Huo R., Yang ZH., Liu JX., 2013. Fire and Explosion Prevention and Control Engineering, Machiney Industry Press, CHINA. Rong-jun SI, PhD, researcher, is majoring in fire explosion prevention technology and equipment research and development work. the heat loss rate, which keeps the temperature rising. The combustion temperature changes in the chamber as shown in Fig. 6 and Fig. 7. 634 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 211 (2018) 629â€“634 Rong-jun SI et al. / Procedia Engineering 00 (2017) 000â€“000 Fig. 6. The temperature curve of CO2 in test 1 Fig. 7. The temperature curve of CO2 in test 2 3.4. Analysis on fire suppression and explosion suppression of self-ignition of lithium ion battery Because the battery burns out the oxygen, the suffocation method does not prevent the lithium ion batteries from burning, and the internal chemical reaction of the battery is less affected by the fire extinguishing agent, the HFC-227ea chemical reaction based on the chain reaction fails to prevent the battery combustion reaction. However, the cooling mechanism of the two kinds of inhibitors play a certain role i

head.ce:title: Experimental Study on Fire and Explosion Suppression of Self-ignition of Lithium Ion Battery

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd, Chongqing 400037, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: China Coal Science and Engineering Group Chongqing Research Institute Co Ltd

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This study adopted the external heating method to generate the lithium ion battery spontaneous combustion, spraying HFC-227ea and CO2 to conduct fire suppression explosion test, and researched the explosion suppression effect of the gas suppression on lithium ion battery. The results show that HFC-227ea and CO2 mainly inhibit the explosion of the lithium ion battery through the method of cooling. The HFC-227ea and CO2 can extinguish the open flame, reduce the combustion temperature and weaken the explosive strength. The combustion temperature of lithium ion battery undergoes three stages: initial growth, sharp rise and decay. It is necessary to take measures to extinguish fire and explosion in the initial growth stage.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Evaluation of Combustibility of Lithium Ion Battery Components and Dangers They Involve
Research of Explosion Mechanism of Lithium-Ion Battery

ce:other-ref.ce:textref: Ping P., 2014. Lithium Ion Battery Thermal Runaway and Fire Risk Analysis and The Development on The Safer Battery System, University of Science and Technology of China.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Experimental Study on Suppression of Gasoline-air Mixture Explosion in Narrow and Long Confined Space by Heptafluoropropane
Fire and Explosion Prevention and Control Engineering

sb:reference.sb:comment: Rong-jun SI PhD, researcher, is majoring in fire explosion prevention technology and equipment research and development work.
